Football.title: Danny Welbeck LEAPFROGS 6ft3in teammate in stunning goal celebration vs Liverpool after missing out on England squad
Football.content:
DANNY WELBECK may be 35, but he still has the athleticism of a footballer in his 20s.

The Brighton forward did a sensational leapfrog over his team-mate Lewis Dunk after scoring the winning goal in their 2-1 win over Liverpool in Saturday’s early kick-off.





Welbeck initially looked unsure if he strayed offside, but the goal was awarded after a quick VAR check which cued the celebrations.

Putting his hands on the shoulder of the 6ft3in-tall defender, Welbeck launched himself into the air with his arms raised in jubilation.

With a brace, Welbeck took his tally to 12 Premier League goals for the season, raising eyebrows as to whether he should have been included in Thomas Tuchel‘s 35-man England squad.

Fans have been quick to notice Welbeck’s celebration.

One wrote on X: “Did you see that jump by Welbeck just after the VAR”

Another joked: “Welbeck jumped over Dunk after dunking on Liverpool”

The veteran striker began his career at Manchester United’s famed Carrington academy, and was introduced into the first team under Sir Alex Ferguson‘s tutelage in 2008.

After a loan spell with Sunderland, he joined Arsenal for five seasons before moving onto Watford.



BEST ONLINE CASINOS – TOP SITES IN THE UK

At Brighton, Welbeck has netted 45 league goals in six Premier League seasons.

His last call-up for England came in October 2019, a year after he went to Russia as part of their 23-man World Cup squad.

He has scored 16 goals in 42 appearances for England, the most recent one in a 2-0 friendly win over Costa Rica in June 2018.

Football.title: YouTube star KSI mobbed by fans as Andy Carroll prepares for shock managerial debut against Premier League legend
Football.content:
KSI has been mobbed by fans before new side Dagenham and Redbridge play Torquay United.

The famous YouTube owner sacked manager Lee Bradbury earlier this week and appointed former Premier League striker Andy Carroll as interim.





The 42-year-old’s first task as boss was at home to Torquay, who have veteran boss Neil Warnock in charge.

Videos show KSI, aka JJ, attracting the attention of young supporters before the game on Saturday.

More to follow… For the latest news on this story keep checking back at The Sun Online

Football.title: Man Utd flop rages at referee as teammate is booked over never-before-seen corner stunt… but all is not as it seems
Football.content:
ANTONY was left raging at a referee after his team-mate was booked following a never-before-seen corner routine.

The former Manchester United flop was in action for new home Real Betis in the Europa League when the incident occurred.





Betis were 4-1 up on aggregate in the dying minutes of their round of 16 clash against Panathinaikos when Ezequiel Avila was booked.

The forward had faked a run-up on the corner, giving the impression he was going to take it with his right foot.

However, he overran the ball only to switch back and whip in a cross on his left foot.

The cross was met in the air by Natan, who headed home to seemingly give Betis their fifth goal of the night.

The unique corner routine was ruled out by German referee Tobias Stieler who then came over to book Avila.

Antony, 26, was close to his team-mate and could be seen fuming at the decision by the official.

He pleaded with both the referee and the linesman as he raged at the booking.



But the goal was never going to stand as the whistle had already been blown moments before the cross reached Natan.

SUN VEGAS WELCOME OFFER: GET £50 BONUS WHEN YOU JOIN

Stieler was more focused on cautioning Avila due to time-wasting and not the deceiving play.

The whistle was heard by the Greek side’s players who had remained static in the box as the ball found the net.

Antony was able to have some enjoyment in the game, having netted his side’s fourth.

He pulled out a unique celebration as he put on a tweed flat cap during the jubilations.



Betis have now advanced to the quarter-finals and will face Portuguese side Braga for a spot in the last four.

Antony joined the LaLiga side permanently in the summer of 2025 after an initial loan in the second half of the 2024/25 season.

He made the exit from Old Trafford after a struggling two-and-half-years following his £85.5million switch from Ajax.



The Brazilian has found his best form in Spain, having already scored 21 times in 61 games.

His record is significantly better than his 12 goals in 96 appearances for United.

Football.title: England in major World Cup boost as injured star gets shock call-up 24 hours after Thomas Tuchel’s squad selection
Football.content:
JUDE BELLINGHAM could make his first appearance in over six weeks just days after being named in Thomas Tuchel’s final England squad before the World Cup.

The Real Madrid midfielder has been out with injury since the start of February and was a surprise inclusion in Tuchel’s massive 35-man group named for friendlies against Uruguay and Japan.





But Bellingham, 22, is fit enough to feature for Madrid on Sunday against rivals Atletico, boss Alvaro Arbeloa has revealed.

The Spaniard said: “He’s available, and he’ll be in the squad tomorrow.

“We’ll see if he plays. I think so. I’m looking forward to seeing him on the pitch.

“He’s an intelligent player, and he knows what to do in every moment. I’m happy he’ll be available and ready to help us tomorrow.”



Bellingham has not played since going off early in a 2-1 win over Rayo Vallecano with a hamstring issue that looked likely to rule him out of international contention until the summer.

He was not involved as Madrid beat Manchester City 2-1 at the Etihad on Tuesday, also missing the first leg and everything over the past month-and-a-half.

Bellingham did travel to England with his team-mates to watch on from the stands.

Three Lions manager Tuchel, 52, who has previously been critical of Bellingham during his reign, was still keen to get him in if possible.



BEST ONLINE CASINOS – TOP SITES IN THE UK

He explained: “Everyone had the same interest, which is not always the case.

“But in this case, we all had the same interest.

“I wanted Jude to be in camp, Jude wanted to be in camp and Real Madrid is happy for him to come because we can provide, basically, team training for him.



“We can provide that, so all boxes are ticked.”

Tuchel’s mammoth playing camp will be split for the two friendlies with outsiders getting the chance to impress at Wembley on Friday against Uruguay.

The ‘regulars’ will then come in for Japan and Tuchel will have the opportunity to chat with Bellingham again.

The German head coach continued: “I think it was very important to get the expectations right — it is maybe also very important to get your expectations right [in the media] about Jude in this camp.

“We will continue his progress in integration into team training.



“We will be very careful and not take any risk with Jude to avoid reinjury.

“It’s a very rare injury for him to have a muscle injury, so he’s not experienced in it, so we will be very, very careful with it and not take any risk.

“The best case scenario is that he gets some minutes against Japan.

“That is the framework and then we will adapt from day to day.”

Real welcome Atletico in a crucial match for their LaLiga title hopes.



RECOMMENDED STORIES

Leaders Barcelona could be seven points clear at the top by kick-off as they welcome Rayo on Sunday afternoon.

The Madrid derby starts at 8pm later that night.

Football.title: What happened in the 100th minute at Bournemouth shows why Man United need three midfielders, not two
Football.content:
MANCHESTER UNITED plan on signing two midfielders in the summer. They need three.

Kobbie Mainoo will inevitably sign a new contract now he is back in the fold and Bournemouth was his 11th successive start.





But Casemiro’s role in United’s dropped points again exposed the gaping chink in United’s armour that their opponents easily exploit.

It was only last week that a few deluded thousand Stretford Enders were chanting “One more year, one more year, Casemiro”. A lot of United fans despaired at that fickleness.

That chant was conspicuous by its absence on the terraces at the Vitality Stadium, where United’s hardcore followers were never going to sound as impressionable as those at Old Trafford had five days earlier.

For the 11th time this season, Casemiro was shown a yellow card. Regular watchers of United know that whenever this happens, he is borderline redundant.



In every game that he has been booked, Casemiro has not finished it. At Bournemouth, he was carded in the 28th minute. His shelf-life should not have extended beyond the hour.

At Liverpool in October, Casemiro was booked in the 57th minute and substituted in the 58th.

Michael Carrick is more positive and proactive with his substitutes but Ruben Amorim was more reactive where Casemiro was concerned.

At Bournemouth, Casemiro plodded on for 82 minutes and by the time his number flashed up, the scoreboard had changed again.



United’s midfield was too loose on Bournemouth’s compact pitch, where they have form for allowing contests to descend into chaos.

Two forward-thinking passes from Marcus Tavernier and Junior Kroupi inside the United third played in Evanilson, who was impeded by Harry Maguire inside the area.

Casemiro followed the red-carded Maguire off the pitch, as Michael Carrick was forced to settle for a point in a match that United had led twice.

United do not have the personnel to see out most games comfortably and keep opponents at arm’s length.



Four of United’s seven wins under Carrick have been by one goal and three of their ten opponents have had red cards to contend with.

Their 2-0 defeat of Manchester City was a rip-roaring, adrenaline-fuelled triumph but 81st-minute goals were required to open up two-goal advantages against Tottenham and Aston Villa.

The hesitancy for Carrick in delaying Casemiro’s withdrawal is doubtless down to the choice of replacement.

Manuel Ugarte is the only other defensive midfielder in the United squad and purportedly Casemiro’s heir. He is more of a spare.



Carrick actually replaced Casemiro with Ugarte on 61 minutes at Newcastle earlier this month. The key context was that United had a man advantage after Jacob Ramsey’s first-half dismissal.

Ugarte is a £50.75million dud. In a season where he has only started nine times, United have conceded 25 goals with him on the pitch, nine of them after he has emerged as a substitute.

So it was a modicum of progress for Ugarte, sent on in the 81st minute, that United did not ship a winning goal. There were nine minutes of added time and the game stretched into the 100th minute.

It was when the game hit that mark that Ugarte brainlessly committed a foul on the touchline just inside the United third.



United were clinging on for a point and a man light. They must have felt like they were reduced to nine men after Ugarte helpfully teed up one more opportunity for Bournemouth.

Casemiro will go in the summer and plenty of United fans would drive Ugarte to Manchester Airport and pay the drop-off charge.

He lost the confidence of Amorim, his coach at Sporting, and has not gained enough trust from Carrick to earn a start.

With over-the-hill and underwhelming midfield protection, United’s defence is shielded by obstacles about as sturdy as papier mache.



Casemiro was once dubbed the “cement between the stones” by Erik ten Hag but he cracked long ago. United’s power brokers have not been hoodwinked by Casemiro’s respectable form this season and are right to release him in June.

Come next season, with European football back on the agenda, United will need at least four midfielders and it feels fanciful that Ugarte will be one of them.

The 24-year-old is two seasons in and the number of truly impressive performances he has produced can be counted on one hand.

Alex Scott, omitted from the England squad, excelled against United, who admire the Jersey-born conductor.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

Scott’s performance was a reminder that United will require silk and steel in the summer; a controller and a combatant in midfield.



That’s two, but they will still need a third.

Football.title: ‘I thought I was a goner’ – Liverpool icon Ian Rush reveals horror near-death illness and how cool-headed wife saved him
Football.content:
IAN RUSH has revealed the terrifying moment he feared he was about to die after a sudden illness left him fighting for his life.

The Reds icon, 64, spent six days in hospital – including time in intensive care – after the shock ordeal of suffering from undiagnosed asthma before partner Carol Anthony came to his rescue.



Rush, Liverpool’s all-time top scorer with a staggering 346 goals in 660 games, admitted the experience has completely changed his outlook on life.



In an interview with The Telegraph, he said: “I had Covid, a superflu and a severe asthma attack.

“I thought I was a goner. It was like I was drowning. I was in a state of panic and Carol, who is a trained lifeguard, kept me breathing, and then the NHS people were brilliant. I was telling myself: ‘No, I’m not ready to go, I need to keep fighting. This can’t be the end.’”

It all started in December when he thought he had come down with a winter bug, but after collapsing he and his partner Carol realised it was more serious.

When Carol called the emergency services and they rushed round, it became apparent it was life threatening.



He is now recovering, but says the scare forced him to confront his own mortality like never before.

Rush added: “When I was recovering, the doctors asked me how long I have been suffering from asthma. I told them I didn’t know I did.

“There are so many things you start thinking about that you never did before – all the things you need to have in place if the worst happens but you would usually put it off.

BEST ONLINE CASINOS – TOP SITES IN THE UK

“I was recommended more sunshine and swimming, and was told to learn how to breathe correctly. I didn’t know I had been doing it wrong.”



The former striker, who formed a legendary partnership with Kenny Dalglish during Liverpool’s dominant era, is now taking daily precautions just to manage his breathing.

Rush now carries an inhaler, takes regular medication and completes breathing exercises three times a day.

Doctors were stunned to discover he had been unknowingly living with asthma.

The near-death experience has also made Rush reflect on his incredible career at Anfield.

Rush was a phenomenon, playing a key role in one of the most dominant periods for a team in English football history.



Between 1972 and 1990, the Reds claimed 11 First Division titles in 18 years – with Rush right at the heart of that golden era, winning five along with two European Cups in 1981 and 1984.

While he has always played down his achievements, the scare has made him think differently.

Rush said: “I’ve spent most of my life downplaying my goal records a bit.



“I’ve always said they are there to be broken, and what mattered most is that the team won trophies. I still believe that.

“There is also a part of me which now thinks if someone wants to ask about my records, why shouldn’t I let them shout about it?”

Rush admitted the outpouring of support he received during his illness also made him realise just how much he means to fans.

Liverpool even stepped in to help his recovery, arranging a four-week break in Dubai so he could benefit from warmer weather.

The Welshman added: “Liverpool got in touch while I was ill and said they wanted to do what they could to help.



“Because they knew warmer weather would aid my recovery, they organised a break in Dubai for four weeks.

“You don’t realise how dangerous cold weather is until you get older. I appreciated everyone’s concern. I’m a lot more comfortable now.”

Even now, Rush can recall almost every goal from his glittering career, including his famous European Cup contributions.

But there is still one that frustrates him.



Rush said: “The penalty in the shoot-out against Roma in 1984 was one of my most important goals.

“I’ve never understood why goals in penalty shoot-outs are not counted.”

These days, Rush remains close to the game as Liverpool ambassador, and his legacy remains.

On Wednesday night during Barcelona‘s 7-2 win over Newcastle, TNT Sports commentary team Adam Summerton and Glenn Hoddle compared his ruthless finishing to that of Robert Lewandowski as the Polish superstar notched a brace.



But after his health scare, football has taken a back seat for Rush.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

Now, it’s about enjoying life and making sure everything is in order.

Rush admitted: “I told myself I need to write a will. And now I’ve said that, it reminds me… I better go and do it.”

Football.title: Man Utd files: JJ Gabriel could be fast-tracked on to pre-season tour and fans hit by unpopular Old Trafford change
Football.content:
MANCHESTER UNITED could bring several Youth Cup stars on their pre-season Euro tour.

SunSport understands Carrington’s rising stars are poised to join the senior squad’s summer trip.



With senior players delayed by an extended World Cup, youngsters like JJ Gabriel—who has already impressed first-team coaches—are set to be fast-tracked.



Tour plans will undoubtedly take a hit, as the final of the 48-team World Cup showpiece is scheduled for July 19 — a week later than usual.

Players that go beyond the group stage might not return to Manchester until early August, with the Premier League season due to start on August 22.

No matter the manager, Gabriel has left an impact on Ruben Amorim and U18 coach Darren Fletcher, so will likely impress any new arrival in the dugout if Michael Carrick steps down.

Youth Cup star Noah Ajayi and full-back Godwill Kukonki could also be shortlisted for the five friendlies set to be held in European cities.



Shea Lacey, 18, was back in training on Thursday and the forward is another teenager that would surely warrant a spot.

Mikel Arteta brought Arsenal whizkid Max Dowman, then 15, on the Gunners’ tour of Asia last summer.

Dowman has since become the Premier League’s youngest ever scorer after aged 16 and 73 days as the league leaders moved nine points clear of City.

SING WHEN YOU’RE WINNING

Whether it is donning a United tie, a United scarf, cosying up to the manager, hobnobbing with Sir Jim Ratcliffe or referring to them as “we”, Peter Schmeichel continues to do his utmost to atone for playing against United for Manchester City.



United fans of a certain vintage do not think fondly of Schmeichel over his season back in Manchester at Maine Road, where he performed a cartwheel in celebration of City’s first derby victory in 13 years in November 2002.

Schmeichel is a regular at Old Trafford as a pundit for overseas rights holders these days but strikes a partisan note.

The Dane was singing along to the ‘Take Me Home’ anthem shortly before kick off against Aston Villa as he declined a selfie request from a fan.

SITTING DUCKS?

More general admission tickets in front of the directors’ box in the Sir Alex Ferguson Stand are due to be relocated next season to make way for additional hospitality seating.



United matchgoers are becoming numb to such measures. A season-ticket holder who occupies one of the seats that is due to be moved said, “I’m not very happy about it, but they don’t care, do they?”

Those seats, located behind the dugouts, provide one of the best views in the house.

Stuck to the back of them is a sticker that reads, “Please note this is a seated area. Persistent standing will impact the view of your fellow fans and may lead to removal from the stadium.”

There is little danger of that when many of the occupants don half-and-half scarves.



LACKING AMO

It looks like United’s online following may be missing Amorim sound bites.

On the official United YouTube channel, the majority of Amorim’s press conferences racked up views well into six figures, with understandable dips to five figures after a defeat or a draw.

Michael Carrick’s last five press conference videos have five-figure viewings.

Unsurprisingly, Amorim’s post-match appearance at Leeds United – when he effectively engineered his sacking – has not made the United press conference playlist.



PRESSING ISSUE

United’s FA Youth Cup quarter-final against Sunderland drew another small crowd of 2,516 amid a rise in ticket prices for academy games.

It is unclear whether press box attendants count towards the final attendance but, with no official accreditation required, United actually allowed a few fans without laptops into the working area.

WIL-HE-WON’T-HE?

United director of football Jason Wilcox was also present for the Youth Cup tie.

Wilcox, who used to coach the City youth team before he became academy head at the club, is back in United fans’ good books amid their run under Carrick and the success of their summer signings.



Nearly two years on from his arrival at the club, Wilcox is yet to speak with external media.

He embraced a reporter at Selhurst Park after United beat Crystal Palace 2-1. Some have speculated whether that particular reporter will get a one-on-one with Wilcox.

ALL ACADEMIC?

The directors’ box was full of familiar faces again for the Sunderland youth game: Carrick, Jonny Evans, Steve Holland, Jonathan Woodgate, Wayne Rooney, John O’Shea, Darron Gibson, Senne Lammens, Tom Heaton and Graeme Carrick took in the game.

Heaton and Lammens headed for the exit in the 85th minute. Carrick, Evans, Holland and Woodgate soon followed.



REST IN PEACE

There was sad news on Thursday with the death of former United goalkeeping coach Silvino Louro.

Louro was Jose Mourinho’s main goalkeeping coach and assisted him at Porto, Chelsea, Inter Milan, Real Madrid and United.

Mourinho visited Louro in Madrid when he was unwell earlier this season.

YOUTH FILES

On Wednesday night, Jim Thwaites was an unsung hero at Old Trafford in the FA Youth Cup as United claimed a 3-2 win after coming from behind.



United’s forward Noah Ajayi claimed the headlines, but Thwaites put in a solid midfield showing, dictating play throughout.

Thwaites, a clinical No6, has been hailed as a “cheat code” by fans due to his specialism on set pieces, including corners and free kicks.

He is proving himself ready to be that fifth man in the midfield department next season.

WHEELIE KIND

United have put on free coach travel for supporters making the trek to Bournemouth tonight.

Four hundred fans have accepted the club’s generous offer for another inconveniently-timed kick-off in Dorset.



RECOMMENDED STORIES

In Bournemouth’s nine seasons in the Premier League, they have hosted United for a Saturday 3pm kick-off only once.

United have had four lunchtime kick-offs at the Vitality Stadium, two tea-time games and tonight is their second evening timeslot.

Football.title: Liverpool suffer major injury blow as star forward forced off moments after delayed kick-off at Brighton
Football.content:
HUGO EKITIKE lasted just SEVEN minutes before he was substituted with an injury in Saturday’s early kick-off at Brighton.

The French forward collided with with James Milner in the opening minutes and needed treatment from the physio.





Ekitike tried continuing on, but was visibly distraught with tears in his eyes after realising he had to come off the pitch.

While the nature of his issue remains unclear, replays show it could have been a dead leg; a common muscle-to-bone impact injury in contact sports.

Arne Slot sent on Curtis Jones as his replacement with Cody Gakpo moving into the lone striker position.

It is yet another injury blow for Slot who was already without goalkeeper Alisson Becker and star forward Mohamed Salah.



Shortly later, Brighton took the lead through Danny Welbeck in the 14th minute.

But Liverpool equalised on the half hour mark after Lewis Dunk‘s mistake allowed Milos Kerkez to score his second Premier League goal for the Merseyside club.

With a two-week international break before Liverpool’s next fixture, Slot will be hoping to have his star forward back before their FA Cup quarter-final against Manchester City on April 4.

Four days later, they travel to Paris to face Paris Saint-Germain in a crucial Champions League quarter-final first leg clash.



BEST ONLINE CASINOS – TOP SITES IN THE UK

Long-term absentee Alexander Isak, who has been ruled out since December with a broken leg, could return to face the French giants.

Should Ekitike be ruled out for a lengthy period, Liverpool’s trophy hopes will suffer another huge blow in what has been a disappointing campaign.

Football.title: Liverpool get red card verdict as Steven Gerrard weighs in on Brighton controversy
Football.content:
Liverpool get red card verdict as Steven Gerrard weighs in on Brighton controversy

Liverpool were furious that Brighton winger Yankuba Minteh was not shown a second yellow card for diving after going down under pressure from Jeremie Frimpong in the box

View 2 Images Yankuba Minteh went over in the box looking for a penalty (Image: AFP/Getty Images)

Liverpool legend Steven Gerrard believes that Brighton winger Yankuba Minteh should have been shown a second yellow card for diving on Saturday. Arne Slot was furious after referee Darren England failed to take action against Minteh in the first half.

‌



Minteh had already been booked in the Premier League's early kick-off when he threw himself to the floor. Brighton played the ball in behind Jeremie Frimpong and Minteh raced to get there first before flopping to the ground.

‌



England waved away the Brighton winger's appeal for a penalty, but also didn't take action for diving. Slot was then seen screaming at the referee while making a diving motion with his hands.

‌



Liverpool went into the break level at 1-1 with the Seagulls, but had salted rubbed in the wound shortly after half-time when Minteh provided the cross that saw Danny Welbeck score his second goal and put Brighton ahead for the second time.

FOLLOW OUR LIVERPOOL FB PAGE! Latest Reds news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

Brighton vs Liverpool kick-off delayed as Premier League issue statement READ MORE :

Asked at half-time on TNT Sports if Minteh should have been sent off, Gerrard said: “Well I think he’s extremely lucky. It has been a great battle down that side - they’ve both got blistering pace. The ball gets played in behind here, there’s no contact and he just goes over.

‌



"It is a dive in my opinion and he gets away with one here. He’s very very lucky. I’ll be surprised if the manager keeps him on much longer actually with [Kaoru] Mitoma on the bench, so keep an eye out for that as well.”

Ex-Liverpool striker Peter Crouch then added: “I agree with Arne Slot there. It was obvious that he was looking for the penalty and he quite easily could have gone there.”

View 2 Images Arne Slot belived that Yankuba Minteh had dived

‌



Welbeck put Brighton ahead with a close-range header in the 14th minute before Lewis Dunk's error allowed Liverpool to equalise. The Brighton centre-back flicked on a long ball towards his goalkeeper, only for Milos Kerkez to read it and lob Bart Verbruggen for 1-1.

After Minteh avoided a red card - and Fabian Hurzeler opted against replacing him - Brighton took the lead again in the 56th minute. The winger sent in a delicious cross for Jack Hinshelwood, who cushioned it back into the path of Welbeck to side-foot him.

Minteh continued to be a threat down the left wing for Brighton, forcing Giorgi Mamardashvili into a save one-on-one minutes later after going through on goal.

Football.title: Steven Gerrard questions Trent Alexander-Arnold England snub and makes World Cup claim
Football.content:
Steven Gerrard questions Trent Alexander-Arnold England snub and makes World Cup claim

Real Madrid star Trent Alexander-Arnold was left out of Thomas Tuchel's England squad for friendlies against Uruguay and Japan with Tino Livramento and Djed Spence preferred

View 2 Images Liverpool icon Steven Gerrard believes Trent Alexander-Arnold offers something different for England (Image: Getty Images)

Liverpool icon Steven Gerrard has expressed surprise at Trent Alexander-Arnold's omission from England's squad for the upcoming international break. The Real Madrid star was overlooked in Thomas Tuchel's 35-man selection for the friendly matches against Uruguay and Japan.

‌



Tino Livramento and Djed Spence were chosen as England's right-backs, with Reece James sidelined due to injury. Former Liverpool ace Alexander-Arnold hasn't represented his country since the 1-0 victory over Andorra in June.

‌



On Saturday, when questioned about the right-back's absence, Anfield favourite Gerrard stated his belief that Alexander-Arnold is more than capable of playing. "Very much so," he responded when asked if Tuchel's decision had taken him aback.

‌



England squad named as Thomas Tuchel hands out recalls ahead of World Cup READ MORE :

OPINION Jeremy Cross Ruthless Thomas Tuchel is RIGHT with brutal Trent Alexander-Arnold snub

"Listen, we've got some good options at right-back, Reece James is world class, I really like Livramento, I know he's had a couple of injuries but he can play both sides. I understand the selection, but I just think, 'is Trent among the best 35 players in this country?' Of course he is.

"There's going to be certain games where England have to open the door, they might be against a low block where it's really frustrating and Trent is one of these players who can come off the bench and just completely change the game with one pass, he's that level of a player."

‌



"So I am surprised and I'm disappointed for him. But there's a lot of other situations that you've got to take into consideration. How much has he played? Is he physically at his best? Is there stuff going on off the pitch? Is it where he (Tuchel) doesn't want a group of players where those who aren't going to be in the XI that might be different people around the place...

View 2 Images Trent Alexander-Arnold has been left out of the latest England squad (Image: Getty Images)

"There's a lot that gets taken into consideration before you select a squad across a six-week period rather than for a one-off game."

‌



Tuchel offered an explanation for the decision during his press conference on Friday. "Tough decision for Trent, as it is for Ollie Watkins and Luke Shaw. These decisions come with the job. It's a sporting decision.

"Tino Livramento and Djed Spence can play for us on the right, Dominic Solanke and Dominic Calvert-Lewin can play up front. "I have a very clear picture of what Ollie can give us, so I want to have a look at two other players to get a better picture and have a clearer opinion.

"The right-backs (we have) offer slightly different profiles (to Alexander-Arnold), the evidence is we were good in September-November than anything from Trent.

Article continues below

"I played many times against him and suffered many times when he played against my teams. I know very well his strengths and what he can give."

Football.title: Roy Keane named Man Utd manager he wants as new Michael Carrick rant unleashed
Football.content:
Roy Keane named Man Utd manager he wants as new Michael Carrick rant unleashed

Ex-Manchester United star remains unconvinced by Michael Carrick and has named his choice for Ruben Amorim's successor

View 3 Images Roy Keane has already revealed his choice for United's next manager (Image: Sky Sports)

Ex-Manchester United skipper Roy Keane reckons Newcastle manager Eddie Howe would be the perfect managerial choice for the club. Michael Carrick has surpassed all expectations since his return to Old Trafford on an interim basis in mid-January after Ruben Amorim's dismissal.

‌



The former United midfielder has orchestrated an impressive spell of results that has lifted the club to third in the Premier League standings and positioned them favourably to qualify for the Champions League next season. United were held to a 2-2 stalemate by Bournemouth on Friday night, but still sit third in the table, and will do so whatever happens elsewhere this weekend.

‌



Keane recently insisted that Carrick, 44, shouldn't be handed the permanent United manager's role, though. It seems the ex-Republic of Ireland international has already settled on his preferred candidate, with his remarks advocating for Howe to take the reins at the club now coming back into focus.

‌



Speaking on Sky Sports in January, Keane said: "I'd go with Eddie Howe. I like him. I like what he's done. He's managed a lot of games. When his teams are at it, they'll play good football. He has his critics, but I like what he's done at Newcastle.

FOLLOW OUR MAN UTD FB PAGE! Latest United news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

Man Utd to fire complaint to PGMOL over Bournemouth VAR drama with club raging READ MORE :

"He's managed seven or 800 games. He's still a young man. I love his calmness. Maybe Man Utd need a little bit of that. We've seen with our previous [appointments], we like people with emotions, but he's got that calmness. The job he's done at Newcastle, Champions League and winning a cup, I'd be happy to see him go in there."

‌



Despite Carrick's impressive track record since assuming control at United, claiming seven victories, two draws, and suffering just one defeat from his ten matches in charge, Keane remains unconvinced by his former teammate in the United dugout.

When questioned whether he would hand Carrick the United manager's position permanently this summer following United's recent 3-1 triumph over Aston Villa, Keane told Sky Sports: "No, I think there are better options out there.

View 3 Images Michael Carrick has performed well as United's interim boss (Image: Charlotte Wilson/Offside/Offside via Getty Images)

‌



"I think the games that he has come in, he has won football matches and done a very good job. He has simplified things, but there has been no jeopardy in those games. They've had free weeks to prepare for those matches, it has been good timing for him, and he has done a very good job. I take my hat off to him.

"And if he does get the job in the summer, good luck to him. But I think there are far better options out there for the next manager of Man United.

"If Carrick had been mentioned three months ago as the next United manager you'd have been locked up. He wasn't even in the reckoning! But with time he has got the opportunity and he has done a very good job. I just think there are better options out there.

‌



"Most players do like a caretaker manager. It's different when he comes in and has to make a decision about contracts, pre-season and medical staff, transfers and recruitment.

"I think to manage Manchester United, you need someone a lot more experienced in terms of winning trophies, competing in Europe and he hasn't got that. He might have that in a few years, and he might be in the mix then, but the fact he's winning football matches at the moment of course gives him a great chance.

View 3 Images Newcastle United head coach Eddie Howe speaks to the media (Image: Newcastle United via Getty Images)

‌



"So everyone else can get giddy about Man United being third in the league but I don't. I'm not one of them. I don't get all excited for stuff like that. I get excited when teams are winning trophies and competing week in, week out. So, I'm not one to get too giddy."

Howe is understood to be pocketing roughly £6million per year in the North East and to possess at least three years left on his current deal. Any prospective suitor would probably need to stump up a substantial eight-figure amount to trigger his departure.

Questioned in October 2025, amidst speculation linking him to United, Howe restated his dedication to the Magpies. He said: "I'm all in. My commitment here is 100 per cent here and always has been. My family is still here, and we are fighting every day to make a success of what we are doing."

Football.title: Rio Ferdinand gives green light to £80m Man Utd transfer as deal 'accepted'
Football.content:
Rio Ferdinand gives green light to £80m Man Utd transfer as deal 'accepted'

Manchester United have put a new midfielder right at the top of their summer shopping list ahead of the transfer window and Rio Ferdinand is a big fan of one of their potential targets

View 2 Images Rio Ferdinand has given his verdict on Man United's transfer approach (Image: Getty Images)

Rio Ferdinand believes Elliot Anderson ‘ticks Manchester United’s boxes’ as they continue their hunt for a new midfielder. The Red Devils have put a new central midfielder right at the top of their summer shopping list.

‌



Their need is now higher, with Casemiro set to depart at the end of the season at the conclusion of his deal. A number of names have already been linked with potential moves to Old Trafford including Brighton’s Carlos Baleba, Nottingham Forest star Anderson, as well as Newcastle duo Sandro Tonali and Bruno Guimaraes.

‌



But there is a clear front-runner in the view of former United defender Ferdinand. He said: “Beginning of the season everyone was talking about [Carlos] Baleba, he seems to have gone off the boil a little bit form wise.

‌



“Elliot Anderson, he seems to have jumped to the front of the queue. I think he’s a wonderful player. I think Elliot Anderson is the right type not only of player but character, I think he’d be a good addition to the squad.

FOLLOW OUR MAN UTD FB PAGE! Latest United news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

Man Utd to fire complaint to PGMOL over Bournemouth VAR drama with club raging READ MORE :

Fuming Michael Carrick calls out referee for 'astonishing' decision in Man Utd draw READ MORE :

“Tonali is a big talent that everyone is talking about, Guimaraes is another big talent that everyone is talking about, but I just think the type of midfielder United need is somebody who is a bit more all-action, who can cover ground, who is dynamic, who is robust, strong, who can drive a team.

‌



“Right now Anderson seems to be ticking those boxes. But I think there are a few other young guns out there who I won’t mention their names yet because I don’t want anyone else to get on that scent.”

Anderson, 23, joined Forest from Newcastle in the summer of 2024 and he has gone on to make 82 appearances for the Midlands side. He has also kicked off his international career over recent seasons, with six caps for England.

View 2 Images Man United are among the clubs interested in Elliot Anderson (Image: Getty Images)

‌



The midfielder remains under contract with Forest until 2029. It has previously been reported that the club want to bank around £80million should they choose to sell Anderson, a price that United have accepted they will have to pay in order to land one of their top targets.

"I'm just focusing on my football at the moment and performing and trying to give everything for Nottingham Forest, really," Anderson said earlier this season when asked about his future. "So that's all that's on my mind."

Football.title: Alan Shearer backs Man Utd's VAR complaint as he fumes at 'terrible standard'
Football.content:
Alan Shearer backs Man Utd's VAR complaint as he fumes at 'terrible standard'

Manchester United will write to the PGMOL after being left furious with the decisions in their draw at Bournemouth and Alan Shearer feels their pain over the officiating

View 2 Images Alan Shearer has slammed the quality of Premier League officiating (Image: The Rest is Football podcast)

Alan Shearer has called out the "terrible" standard of refereeing in the Premier League after Manchester United became the latest club to be incensed by VAR's inconsistencies. United plan to make a complaint to the PGMOL after feeling they were on the receiving end of a poor decision in their 2-2 draw with Bournemouth on Friday night.

‌



Michael Carrick described it as “mental” and “astonishing” that referee Stuart Attwell and the VAR didn't award a penalty for Adrien Truffert's challenge on Amad. Attwell waved away the appeal and Bournemouth went straight up the other end to make it 1-1 through Ryan Christie.

‌



United went 1-0 up from the penalty spot after Alex Jimenez pulled back Matheus Cunha, while Harry Maguire was shown a straight red card for barging over Evanilson in the box. But Truffert's tug on Amad was "not sufficient for a foul", according to the Premier League match centre.

‌



Bruno Fernandes felt VAR should have overturned Attwell's original decision and United are going to write to PGMOL chief Howard Webb to make their feelings felt. Shearer has been one of VAR's harshest critics and he backed up United's anger on Saturday morning.

FOLLOW OUR MAN UTD FB PAGE! Latest United news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

Man Utd held by Bournemouth as Harry Maguire goes from hero to villain - 5 talking points READ MORE :

Man Utd to fire complaint to PGMOL over Bournemouth VAR drama with club raging READ MORE :

"You remember all those years ago when it was wheeled out to say that they are not going to re-referee and it was only for clear and obvious things, but it could not have gone further south than that even if they tried," the Premier League legend said on BBC Radio 5 Live.

‌



"I just think it’s been terrible and the standard of referees because of VAR are the worst we have seen for a long time and they are only getting worse.

"Last night was a poor decision from it. You should have given a penalty when you have already given two like that. How on earth do you not give another one? They are too reliant on it and it’s affecting the standard of refereeing now, and it’s not a good look."

View 2 Images Bruno Fernandes was unhappy with Stuart Attwell's decisions (Image: Rob Newell/Getty Images)

‌



Ex-Liverpool defender Stephen Warnock the added: "I think it’s [VAR] the worst thing that has been introduced to the game. I think it has ruined it. It’s ruined the enjoyment in stadiums. I think it’s ruined it for referees to officiate. I think that it’s a little bit of a safety blanket every time they referee.

"You talked about Brighton winning four out of five, they should have got a draw against Arsenal, and, PGMOL have come out and said they made a mistake and Brighton should have had a penalty. So VAR isn’t perfect, it isn’t great, it makes mistakes and it’s still subjective which is a huge problem, and I just think it’s a poor addition to the game."

Football.title: Liverpool star Hugo Ekitike in tears hobbling off vs Brighton as injury worries deepen
Football.content:
Liverpool's injury problems continued to mount on Saturday afternoon as Hugo Ekitike was forced off inside eight minutes of the Reds' Premier League clash against Brighton & Hove Albion. The striker started at the AMEX Stadium after scoring in midweek against Galatasaray.

However, he stayed down after colliding with ex-Liverpool man James Milner before receiving treatment. He returned to the field very briefly, before going down again and being replaced by Curtis Jones.

Less than ten minutes later, Arne Slot's men fell behind when Danny Welbeck converted from close range. The injury to Ekitike comes after Liverpool were forced to name a squad without Mohamed Salah.

The forward was confirmed as an absentee for the trip to the AMEX Stadium after being forced off against Galatasaray in the Champions League on Wednesday night. "Indeed, [it's] unusual," Slot said when speaking about the Egyptian's fitness. "As a result of that I think you can expect the outcome. So, [he's] not available for tomorrow."

He added: "The good thing for Liverpool and for us is that we go to an international break. The bad thing for Egypt is that he can't go there. We are hoping also with what Mo has shown in the past that he can recover faster than other players might in similar situations because he takes such good care of his body.

Image: Liverpool FC via Getty Images) Liverpool FC via Getty Images)

"History has shown that he can be earlier back than some others. But it's only two weeks when we go again so let's hope in that period of time he can be back."

Salah joins Alisson Becker as a recent injury problem for Liverpool after he picked up a muscular injury. The goalkeeper missed the midweek win and isn't expected back until after the international break.

Taking to Instagram on Saturday morning, Alisson gave an update on his injury. "Unfortunately I will be out of action for a while," he said. "But already working to come back even stronger! Thank you for the support!"

Alexander Isak has been out since December and is closing on a return to fitness. Wataru Endo and Giovanni Leoni are long-term absences, while Conor Bradley is out with a serious knee injury.

Football.title: David Moyes calls out Premier League chiefs for controversial Chelsea fine
Football.content:
David Moyes calls out Premier League chiefs for controversial Chelsea fine

Chelsea were recently hit with a multi-million pound fine by the Premier League for breaching transfer rules but some top-flight bosses are not totally satisfied with the decision

View 2 Images David Moyes has questioned why Chelsea were only fined (Image: Getty Images)

David Moyes has demanded an explanation from the Premier League about a lack of a points deduction for Chelsea. The Blues were recently hit with a £10.75million fine, a suspended transfer embargo and an academy transfer ban for engaging in “deception and concealment” when making illicit payments.

‌



Chelsea self-reported themselves under their new owners, with the payments having been made under former owner Roman Abramovich.

‌



But Everton boss Moyes has been left baffled and has called for the authorities to release a more detailed explanation of Chelsea’s punishment.

‌



Speaking ahead of his side’s clash with the Blues this weekend, he said: “I don’t think they have explained it well enough in the reasoning what the fine was and why it was. I think they have to explain exactly what has happened here. If they don’t then we are never going to understand their reasoning why.

FOLLOW OUR CHELSEA FB PAGE! Latest Blues news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

Enzo Fernandez undermines Liam Rosenior again with brutal Chelsea manager comments READ MORE :

Premier League clubs to vote on handing VAR more power after World Cup decision READ MORE :

“This is me not being anything against Chelsea, absolutely not, I’m just saying that everybody would like to know; Everton supporters, for the pain they had to go through when they had a huge points deduction, and other clubs as well.

‌



“Those points deductions may well have been correct, I don’t know, but we need to see what is the difference? What would you rather have, a £10m fine or a 10 point deduction? The money you get for your league place now, that might cover it.

“It would be good if we could get more of an explanation. How much is the £10m fine having an effect, really?”

Everton have of course been in the Premier League’s crosshairs in the past, having been deducted 10 points in November 2023 for breaching PSR rules, that eventually being reduced to six. The Toffees were then deducted a further two.

‌



View 2 Images Liam Rosenior has already spoken out on Chelsea's fine (Image: Getty Images)

Midlands sides Nottingham Forest and Leicester City have also been hit with points deductions for PSR breaches. Chelsea boss Rosenior has already spoken out on Chelsea’s punishment and claims it isn’t a distraction for his side.

He said: “No, it's not a negative distraction. I think actually that's a line drawn through that issue. We can move on and we can plan to make this club as strong as possible in the long term. That's the idea from the ownership, myself and everybody involved with the club."

Article continues below

Chelsea currently sit sixth in the table ahead of the clash with Everton at Hill Dickinson Stadium but are just three points off of the top four.

Football.title: 'I make Carlos Alcaraz and Jannik Sinner uncomfortable – their dominance is boring'
Football.content:
'I make Carlos Alcaraz and Jannik Sinner uncomfortable – their dominance is boring'

The tennis star has claimed he can end the current dominance of Carlos Alcaraz and Jannik Sinner

View 2 Images Holger Rune has made claims about Alcaraz and Sinner's dominance of the game (Image: Getty)

Danish tennis sensation Holger Rune has declared that the recent supremacy of Carlos Alcaraz and Jannik Sinner is "boring". The 22-year-old also reckons Alcaraz and Sinner find it "uncomfortable" to play him and has complete faith that he can beat the pair when he next meets them on court.

‌



The former world No. 4 has been sidelined since October when he went under the knife for Achilles surgery, having sustained the serious injury at the Stockholm Open in October 2025.

‌



Spanish sensation Alcaraz and his Italian counterpart Sinner have claimed the past nine Grand Slam titles in the sport. The last player to lift a slam outside of this duo was Novak Djokovic, who triumphed at the US Open in 2023.

‌



While Alcaraz and Sinner appear set to dominate the tennis world for years ahead, the outspoken Rune does boast a 2-2 record against Alcaraz and has prevailed in two of his five encounters against Sinner.

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! All the best sports news and much more on our dedicated Facebook page

Speaking to the Tales from Tennis podcast, Rune insisted that his playing style gives him the conviction that he can threaten the duo's grip when he returns to the tour.

‌



Tennis icon forced to hand back prize money and slapped with four-year ban READ MORE :

Carlos Alcaraz hates playing 'nightmare' Brit whose Wimbledon antics attracted fury READ MORE :

"It gives me the belief that I know I can do it, and every time I step [on court] against them, I know I have a chance to beat them. And I know that both of them find it uncomfortable to play me, so that's extra motivation for me," Rune said.

"And yeah, I always had great matches with Jannik, even before he turned out as a Grand Slam champion; he was always an incredible player. I think it's always been an exciting matchup with me and both of them.

"So, for my case, it's about being as consistent as to do it every week to give myself the chance to play them much more often.

‌



"And I think that would also make my level increase a lot, because they get to challenge themselves almost every week against each other.

"With just two guys making finals and winning everything, it gets boring to be honest. I think there needs to be more guys to challenge them, not just one tournament, but consistently."

View 2 Images Carlos Alcaraz and Jannik Sinner have won every Grand Slam since the US Open in 2023 (Image: Getty)

Article continues below

The five-time ATP Tour title winner also revealed he is targeting a return to his former peak, despite his problematic Achilles injury. "My mind hasn't really changed on what my ultimate goal is. It's really the same as it's always been: to be at the top of the game and to win Grand Slam titles," he said.

"And I know I can do it. I just need to be clear in my mind and to be aware of what I have to do, and work on myself physically and on the tennis court. And then I'm able to achieve it." He also revealed that he's targeting a return either at the tail end of the clay season or during the grass campaign."

He added: "My rehab is going well, it's moving in the right direction, and I started to run yesterday, so that's a really nice milestone for me. Moving forward it looks great, it's feeling great, so I'm very positive about everything and hopefully soon I can be back on the court."

Football.title: Greaves' INCREDIBLE streak of 114 wins ended by Sherrock in Women's Series
Football.content:
Beau Greaves' astonishing run of 114 consecutive wins on the PDC Women's Series circuit came to an end with a 4-1 defeat against Fallon Sherrock.

The three-time WDF World Darts Championship kicked off the tournament with a perfect weekend in Germany last month when she made it 113 wins on the spin and 17 consecutive titles.

Greaves, who made history last month when became the first woman to hit a nine-darter on the PDC ProTour, was looking to extend her streak but Sherrock averaged 102.12 to defeat Greaves 4-1 and reach the last 32 of Event Five in Wigan on Saturday.

Longest winning streaks in the PDC Women's Series

114 - Beau Greaves



70 - Beau Greaves



41 - Beau Greaves



29 - Fallon Sherrock



21 - Beau Greaves



20 - Beau Greaves



19 - Beau Greaves



18 - Beau Greaves



17 - Lisa Ashton



17 - Fallon Sherrock (twice)



16 - Lisa Ashton



Image: Fallon Sherrock ended Greaves' incredible streak in Event Five

After beating Lena Welc to extend her winning run to 114 matches, 'Beau 'n' Arrow' was drawn to face Sherrock.

'The Queen of the Palace' was in red-hot form, winning a 13-darter and two 14-dart legs, en route to sealing victory in nine minutes to end Greaves' incredible streak.

Sherrock went on to reach the final where was was beaten by veteran Lisa Ashton 5-3.

Greaves, a two-time women's World Matchplay champion, last tasted defeat on the Women's Series circuit against Gemma Hayter in April 2025.

The top three players from the final PDC Women's Series rankings will qualify to compete in the 2026/27 Paddy Power World Darts Championship, while the top eight players on the rolling 12-month rankings will compete in July's Betfred Women's World Matchplay.

Football.title: Millwall hit back to hold Ipswich in key promotion clash
Football.content:
Josh Coburn earned what could prove to be a priceless point with a second-half equaliser as Millwall recorded a 1-1 Championship draw against promotion-rivals Ipswich at Portman Road.

The Tractor Boys took the lead late in the first half through Jack Clarke with visitors responding early in the second period through Coburn for a share of the spoils.

The result kept the two teams in third and fourth respectively, two points behind second-placed Middlesbrough, who drew 0-0 at Blackburn, but with Ipswich having a game in hand.

Clarke weaved his way through the centre of the pitch in the third minute and fed Ben Johnson on the left, whose fierce cross into the penalty area failed to find an Ipswich attacker.

Moments later, on-loan Sunderland midfielder Dan Neil went close to opening the scoring with a shot which skimmed past Millwall goalkeeper Anthony Patterson.

At the other end, Christian Walton had to parry away a chance from Camiel Neghli as the Lions countered down the left.

The ball had to be cleared off the Millwall goal line in the ninth minute as Town won a free-kick down the left where Johnson delivered a cross and Darnell Furlong made good contact with the ball but visiting captain Jake Cooper was able to clear.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Jack Clarke found space on the edge of the Millwall penalty area and drilled a low shot past Anthony Patterson.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Camiel Neghli produced a wonderful cross into the Ipswich area and Josh Coburn hammered home a leveller from close range.

Ipswich were having the lion's share of possession and Patterson fumbled the ball following a long-range effort from Johnson but there was no home threat to follow up the rebound.

The Lions shot stopper was called into action moments later when an acrobatic effort from Ivan Azon was unbelievably tipped wide following another cross from Johnson during a pulsating opening 30 minutes.

But leading scorer Clarke gave the Tractor Boys a deserved opening goal in the 41st minute. He picked up the ball just outside the penalty area from Azor Matusiwa and weaved his way into a shooting position, firing low and hard past a despairing and unsighted Patterson from 25 yards.

Millwall enjoyed a perfect start to the second half when they equalised through Coburn in the 50th minute.

Neghli muscled Furlong off the ball, his cross evaded Ipswich defender Jacob Greaves and allowed the Millwall striker to fire home from close range.

The ball was in the back of the Ipswich net again but referee Michael Salisbury ruled that Town goalkeeper Walton was fouled on the goal line by Coburn.

Furlong powered a header just past the post from a corner and from another set-piece Neil's shot from the edge of the penalty area took a deflection, which carried the ball just past the post.

Millwall struck the crossbar late on through substitute Mihailo Ivanovic - following a goalmouth scramble - as the visitors sought the winning goal but the two sides settled for a point apiece.

Ipswich's Kieran McKenna:

Football.title: Middlesbrough frustrated again in promotion pursuit as they are held by Blackburn
Football.content:
Middlesbrough wasted an opportunity to tighten their grip on second place as they were held to a 0-0 draw at Blackburn.

With fellow promotion hopefuls Ipswich and Millwall also cancelling each other out in a 1-1 stalemate, Kim Hellberg's side had the chance to steal a march on their rivals but it was a familiar story as they failed to capitalise on their dominance.

Boro bossed the first half and should have been a couple of goals to the good, but Aidan Morris passed up a gilt-edged early chance and David Strelec's effort at the end of the half was wonderfully saved by Balazs Toth.

The pattern continued in the second half but battling Rovers stemmed the tide and, despite being roared on by more than 7,300 fans, Boro could not find a way through.

As a result, it remains as you were in the race for the Premier League but Boro are now winless in three, having scored just once.

By contrast, Blackburn were well worth the draw which could be vital in the relegation shake-up as Michael O'Neill's men made it four points in a week against teams chasing promotion to sit four clear of danger.

No team has taken more points on the road than Middlesbrough, so it was no surprise they started on the front foot and by the time Luke Ayling forced Toth into a fine low save after four minutes, they were well on top.

They should have taken the lead in the 13th minute when a slick move played in Morris on the right but he slammed against the side netting with just Toth to beat.

Alan Browne was then played through by Riley McGree on the half hour as Boro pushed forward again but his shot was blocked.

Blackburn were second best but certainly committed, and Ryoya Morishita gave them a sight of goal two minutes later when a corner was headed into his path and his low strike was parried by Sol Brynn.

Dominant Boro created another glorious chance on the stroke of half-time when Tommy Conway glided into the area and crossed for Strelec, but Toth produced a stunning reflex save to tip over the first-time effort.

Boro picked up where they left off after the restart and carved an opening when Conway crossed from the right for Morris, but he could not generate enough power and it was straightforward for Toth.

Rovers enjoyed a rare foray forward just past the hour when Moussa Baradji found space on the left to drive into the box but was eventually crowded out and poked harmlessly into the side netting.

They continued to frustrate the visitors, whose only other chance arrived five minutes before the end but Browne's shot was blocked and Rovers claimed a deserved share of the spoils.

Football.title: O'Sullivan through to World Open final after deciding-frame thriller
Football.content:
Ronnie O'Sullivan made it through to World Open final after a sensational deciding-frame clearance

Ronnie O'Sullivan made it through to the World Open final after clinching a deciding-frame thriller against Wu Yize.

With O'Sullivan and Wu locked at 5-5 in the race to six frames, 'The Rocket' produced a wonderful 89 counter-clearance from 43-0 down to seal a dramatic 6-5 victory.

The seven-time world champion is through to a 66th ranking final and now has a chance to win a record-extending 42nd ranking title in Sunday's final against Judd Trump or Thepchaiya Un-Nooh over the best of 19 frames.

Ronnie O’Sullivan: The GOAT

🏆 Seven-time World Champion



🏆 Eight-time UK Champion



🏆 Eight-time Masters Champion



🏆 41 Ranking Titles



⭐️ 1,320 Century Breaks



⭐️ 17 Maximum Breaks



⭐️ Highest Break: 153



⏳ Fastest Maximum Break in 5’08’’, unbeaten for 29 years



🐐 The Greatest of All Time



The Essex cueman is into his first ranking final since beating Judd Trump to win the World Grand Prix in January 2024.

"I like my bottle when I'm flowing. I like my bottle anyway really," said O'Sullivan, who could enjoy another landmark moment with victory in Sunday's title match. He would become the first ever player to win a ranking event in his teens, 20s, 30s, 40s and 50s. "At 5-5 when he missed I had a chance on the red I had to go for it. The ball went in, which I couldn't believe, and I made a great clearance.

"I knew that if I got the red I fancied I could clear up. In previous years I was begging my opponent to not miss because I didn't want to embarrass myself. That is different now. I feel a lot happier because I'm in that frame of mind. Even if I miss, at least I fancy it.

"His cue power and snooker brain is great. He has a lot to learn but it is the easy bits he needs to learn. He's like me before I met Ray Reardon. He can pot balls and score really well. I'm telling him he needs to learn the other side of the game."

O'Sullivan's historic 153 break in colours, starting with free ball after a snooker

🟢



⚫️



🔴 🔴 🔴 🔴 🔴 🔴 🔴 🔴 🔴 🔴 🔴 🔴 🔴 🔴 🔴



⚫️ ⚫️ ⚫️ ⚫️ ⚫️ ⚫️ ⚫️ ⚫️ ⚫️ ⚫️ ⚫️ ⚫️ ⚫️



🩷 🩷



🟡 🟢 🟤 🔵 🩷 ⚫️



On Friday, O'Sullivan made the highest break in professional snooker by hitting a 153 at the tournament.

The 50-year-old achieved the feat after leaving quarter-final opponent Ryan Day in a snooker at the start of the opening frame and the Welshman's failed attempt to get out of it handed O'Sullivan a free ball.

That allowed the seven-time world champion to commence his break with the green, followed by his opening black to put him on eight points before even beginning on the reds.

O'Sullivan then reeled off the 15 reds, accompanied by 13 further blacks and two pinks before clearing up the colours to land a record-breaking 153, paving the way for a dominant 5-0 victory.

O'Sullivan, currently ranked world No 12, has signed up to compete in the World Seniors Championship for the first time this year.

O'Sullivan's route to World Open final

Football.title: Getawhisky faces Charisma Cat at Newbury
Football.content:
Getawhisky and Charisma Cat go head-to-head in our feature race from Newbury - live on Sky Sports Racing.

3.00 Newbury - Classy pair Getawhisky and Charisma Cat fancied

The classy duo of Getawhisky and Charisma Cat will be fancied in this Grade 2 BetVictor British EBF "National Hunt" Mares' Novices' Hurdle at Newbury.

The Dan Skelton-trained Getawhisky ran out an impressive winner of a novices' hurdle at Windsor in January before finishing well to claim fourth behind Khrisma at Market Rasen. This step up in distance is sure to suit for a yard still going after every pound to get the trainers' championship over the line.

Charisma Cat and Watamu filled the places that day, but will both have to concede weight to the likely favourite. Charisma Cat has some excellent form including when finishing second to La Conquiere, while Watamu has a similarly progressive profile for Emma Lavelle.

A deep renewal sees lots of alternatives, including the Fergal O'Brien-trained pair Strong Run and Edith Pelham.

3.35 Newbury - Diamond Street and Lover Desbois headline

A typically competitive renewal of this Goffs Hundred Grand Bumper sees Diamond Street headline a field of 20.

Dan Skelton's gelding was a stylish winner of a Doncaster bumper on debut in January and although the form is untested, he should be in the mix under Harry Skelton.

Lover Desbois, fourth behind Bass Hunter on debut here, improved on that to score at Kempton on his next outing and his trainer Harry Derham explained this has been the plan since.

Keltype made all to win at Chepstow on his second start, while Nicky Henderson's filly Madam Speaker is another to note under Sean Bowen.

4.38 Southwell - Last-time winners Gaeli and Fahrenheit Seven clash

A strong Midnite A Next Generation Betting App Handicap is the feature on this eight-race card at Southwell.

Gaeli made a winning debut for Marco Botti and Jack Gilligan when seeing off Fivethousandtoone earlier this month and he must be feared especially if he can step forward despite a 6lbs rise.

Fahrenheit Seven relished the return to the minimum distance when scoring at Newcastle 43 days ago and that form gives him an obvious each-way chance under top pilot Tom Marquand.

Brosay makes appeal on his first start for Charlie Pike and Kylian is another to note on debut for Robert Cowell.

Best of the rest

Returning to handicap company, Storming George will be popular with bettors in the Get Best Odds Guaranteed At BetVictor Novices' Handicap Hurdle at 1.50pm, with the Nicky Henderson-trained Thewoodcorner a likely threat.

Greatwood Gold Cup hero Heltenham looks the one to beat back at Newbury in the 2.25pm BetVictor Home Of The Saturday Superboost Handicap Chase, although Prairie Wolf, Knappers Hill and Twinjets will all fancy their chances in what looks an excellent contest in Berkshire.

At Bangor, Knickerbockerglory and Static are set to lock horns in the feature 3.20pm Virgin Bet Supports Safe Gambling Handicap Hurdle, with Joyeux Machin making an appearance in the Virgin Bet Handicap Chase earlier on the card.

At Newcastle, Rastnet and Alther Walden look the key players in the Midnite: Built For 2026 Not 2006 Handicap before Starglow - who is closely related to Romantic Style - goes to post for the Always Gamble Responsibly At BetMGM Novice Stakes (5.30pm).

Stateside, Fair Grounds plays host to the 10.42pm Grade 2 Louisiana Derby, which features Autobahn and Emerging Market.

Football.title: Brighton 2-1 Liverpool: Premier League – live
Football.content:
3m ago 10.45 EDT FULL TIME: Brighton & Hove Albion 2-1 Liverpool Brighton’s European dream is alive and kicking after their fourth win in five games! Liverpool however won’t be qualifying for the Champions League if this showing is anything to go by. As dismal as Brighton were delightful. Share

4m ago 10.43 EDT 90 min +6: Rutter has the chance to break into the Liverpool box down the left but miscontrols. Does it matter, though? This is all but over. Share

5m ago 10.42 EDT 90 min +5: Chiesa thinks he’s won a corner down the right … but he hasn’t. Some performative complaining to the linesman, but that’s all Liverpool have left. They ran out of ideas long ago. Share

7m ago 10.41 EDT 90 min +4: Ngumoha has been the one bright light for Liverpool, and he threatens to break down the left. He’s brought down in the Willie Young / Paul Allen 1980 FA Cup final style by Wieffer, who goes into the book. Szoboszlai takes the free kick, and loops it straight down Verbruggen’s throat. Share

8m ago 10.40 EDT 90 min +3: Brighton have been magnificent, though. It’ll be four wins from five if they hold out. Share

9m ago 10.39 EDT 90 min +2: Liverpool can barely get out of their final third. Yet another flaccid Premier League performance from Arne Slot’s side. Share

10m ago 10.38 EDT 90 min +1: The first of six additional minutes sees yet another game of pinball break out around Liverpool’s punch-drunk defence, followed by Gomez being replaced by Veltman. Share

11m ago 10.37 EDT 90 min: Ngumoha wins a corner down the left. Szoboszlai hangs it up generously for Verbruggen to claim. Then Chiesa is booked for a cynical tug on Kadioglu. Share

12m ago 10.35 EDT 88 min: According to TNT Sports, Brighton have run five kilometres more than Liverpool today. Arne Slot would be wise not to cite the efforts against Galatasaray as a factor, because TNT also state that his side have been outrun by the opposition in 26 of their previous 30 fixtures. This is not an isolated incident. Share

14m ago 10.34 EDT 87 min: The corner comes in from the left. Liverpool fail to clear it, and Ayari shoots low and hard. Jones blocks. A third goal wouldn’t flatter Brighton at all. View image in fullscreen Yasin Ayari shoots. Photograph: Tony O Brien/Reuters Share Updated at 10.39 EDT

15m ago 10.33 EDT 86 min: Gakpo probes down the left and whips a powerful curler towards the far corner. Verbruggen is behind it all the way. Brighton counter, and Kadioglu wins a corner off Mac Allister. On the touchline, Arne Slot looks defeated and deflated. And so do his team. Share

16m ago 10.32 EDT 85 min: Ngumoha zips with purpose down the left, but Chiesa miscontrols his cutback. Goal kick. Dunk faffs around, and is booked for delaying the restart. Share

17m ago 10.30 EDT 83 min: Rutter and Ayari come on for Hinshelwood and Welbeck. Share

18m ago 10.29 EDT 82 min: Liverpool can’t get anything going at all. If they don’t somehow pull a rabbit from the hat, they’ll have picked up one point from the last nine available, against Wolves, Spurs and Brighton. It’s not exactly top-five form. Arne Slot’s coat hanging on an increasingly shoogly peg. Share

21m ago 10.27 EDT 80 min: The xG tells its own story. Brighton 2.09, Liverpool 0.95. If anything, that stat flatters Liverpool. Meanwhile for those who favour the eye test, Robertson slices a dreadful cross out for a goal kick. Share

22m ago 10.26 EDT 79 min: Liverpool have been hopeless in this second half, and their minds are increasingly frazzled as Brighton press for the killer third. Szoboszlai is booked for a cynical check on Mitoma. Share

23m ago 10.25 EDT 77 min: Mitoma is immediately in the thick of it, twisting his way down the left and past Jones with absurd ease, cutting back for Hinshelwood, who should score but somehow kicks the ball backwards. Liverpool counter through Chiesa, who shoots from a tight angle on the left, his effort blocked well by Van Hecke. Share

24m ago 10.23 EDT 76 min: Brighton also replace Milner with Baleba, while Liverpool hook Kerkez and the lumbering Konate. On come Chiesa and Robertson. Share

26m ago 10.22 EDT 75 min: Minteh, who has caused Liverpool all sorts of bother, is replaced by Mitoma. “Liverpool play like they have pace all over the pitch,” begins Gary Naylor, setting himself up for the punchline: “Which is a problem, because they haven’t.” Share

26m ago 10.21 EDT 73 min: Konate faffs around yet again, and is nearly stripped of possession by Welbeck. The pair fall in a tangled heap, mainly due to Konate slipping over, allowing Mamardashvili to claim. Brighton want a penalty, and you’ve seen them given, but not this time. This entry was brought to you by the Keystone Film Company. Share

30m ago 10.18 EDT 71 min: Minteh gets the better of Konate again down the left. Konate really is having one of his days. He concedes a corner despite having been initially in charge of the situation. The set piece, needlessly conceded, leads to a game of slapstick pinball in the Liverpool area. The visitors are extremely fortunate that the ball doesn’t sit up nicely enough for any Brighton player to take a shot. Liverpool hanging on a bit here. Share

32m ago 10.15 EDT 69 min: Mac Allister is booked for hauling down Van Hecke. And so is Fabian Hürzeler, for demanding the booking with an imaginary card. Share

33m ago 10.15 EDT 68 min: Minteh steams into the Liverpool box down the left. He’s got Gomez in the middle, but takes the shot on himself instead. Straight at Mamardashvili. Share

34m ago 10.14 EDT 67 min: Replays show that Welbeck’s second goal was right on the borderline of being offside. His arm was off, for sure. But you can’t score with that, so it doesn’t count. The rest of him: fine. All good! “Can we ask VAR to review the clear and obvious error that Thomas Tuchel has made in not calling Welbeck up to the England squad?” wonders Tim Talbot. Share

35m ago 10.12 EDT 65 min: Szoboszlai sends the corner in from the left. Verbruggen nearly punches it into his own net. Another corner, this time from the right. Szoboszlai finds Mac Allister at the near post. The former Brighton midfielder heads harmlessly wide. He should have worked the keeper at the very least. Share

37m ago 10.11 EDT 64 min: Ngumoha comes on for Frimpong. Then Jones aims a curler towards the bottom left. Verbruggen stretches to turn around the post. Great play all round. Share

37m ago 10.10 EDT 63 min: Hinshelwood back-flicks the ball onto Gakpo’s arm in the Liverpool box. He claims a penalty but body position and proximity are Liverpool’s friends. Share

39m ago 10.09 EDT 62 min: Gomez curls the free kick powerfully towards the far corner. Mamardashvili sticks out a strong arm to bat it away. Fine save. Share

40m ago 10.08 EDT 61 min: Minteh sashays past Konate down the left with ease. His cutback is cleared, but Minteh is soon coming at them again, Konate wandering around in the confused fashion. He’s having one of those days. Frimpong is forced to bring Minteh down, and he goes into the book. A free kick coming up in a very dangerous position, just outside the Liverpool box, near the left-hand corner. Share

42m ago 10.06 EDT 59 min: Hinshelwood sticks his studs on Jones’s shin. It’s late and Jones isn’t happy. VAR has a look, but takes the Not Enough In It For Me option. Share

43m ago 10.04 EDT 57 min: There’s a lengthy VAR check. Was Welbeck ahead of the ball when Hinshelwood teed him up? The answer eventually comes: nope! Brighton lead again! Share

44m ago 10.03 EDT GOAL! Brighton 2-1 Liverpool (Welbeck 56) … but they’re happy now! Minteh crosses deep from the left. Hinshelwood arrives at the far post and cushions a pass back across the face of goal. Welbeck is on point to turn the ball home! Simple as that. Liverpool’s defending woeful. View image in fullscreen Danny Welbeck scores their second goal. Photograph: Glyn Kirk/AFP/Getty Images Share Updated at 10.06 EDT

Football.title: Fulham v Burnley, Hearts v Dundee and more: Saturday football clockwatch – live
Football.content:
7m ago 10.40 EDT “I wish it was just as easy as throwing six forwards on,” said Scott Parker this week as he tries to balance defensive solidity with actually scoring enough goals to keep Burnley up. Their last few games have seen different approaches – an all-guns-blazing 4-3 defeat to Brentford, a limp 2-0 loss at Everton and a promising-but-toothless 0-0 draw with Bournemouth. “I think we’ve tried that as well in terms of [throwing] caution to the wind,” Parker said yesterday. “They’re the constant decisions you’re trying to make. I wish it was just as easy as throwing six forwards on. “There’s a bit more to it than just that. But for sure we’re at the stage now where we need to get three points. We need to win football matches to give ourselves a chance to stay in this division. Hence why we’ve probably changed a little bit in terms of our structure.” Share

49m ago 09.58 EDT Fulham v Burnley team news Yes, this is the only Premier League offering this afternoon but it’s an extremely important one for Burnley. They need points and they need them now – nine separate them and Nottingham Forest in 17th with only seven games to go after today. Fulham are 12th but only four points behind Brentford in 7th and a European place. Oh, the memories of Bobby Zamora and Zoltan Gera putting Juventus to the sword. Marco Silva has made one change to last weekend’s team that drew 0-0 (uh oh) with Forest – Rodrigo Muniz in for Raúl Jiménez up top. Fulham XI (4-2-3-1): Leno; Castagne, Andersen, Bassey, Robinson; Berge, Iwobi; Wilson, King, Bobb; Muniz

Subs: Lecomte, Diop, Sessegnon, Reed, Lukic, Cairney, Smith Rowe, Chukwueze, Jiménez Burnley are unchanged from their 0-0 draw (oh dear) with Bournemouth last time out. Armando Broja, formerly of Fulham, has to make do with a place on the bench. Football.title: Man City 5-2 Tottenham, London City Lionesses 1-1 Chelsea and more: WSL clockwatch – live
Football.content:
5m ago 10.42 EDT Tottenham head coach Martin Ho tells Sky Sports: “A game of two halves. First half, we didn’t compete at all. Second half, we did compete, better with duels and more organised. We had numerous chances and could have got a few goals but we can’t perform in that way in the first half and expect to come out with more. “First half, we need to make sure that doesn’t happen again for remainder of the season. The first half, we had numerous chances on the break and some chances when we built up but in the second half we had more control with ball and more control in defence.” Share

10m ago 10.37 EDT In another blow to Chelsea fans after today’s result, The Athletic is reporting that Sam Kerr and Millie Bright are expected to leave the club at the end of the season when their contracts expire. Share

18m ago 10.30 EDT Manchester United forward Melvine Malard says: “We deserved that because we started good. Terland scored a goal and it’s not easy but we keep going. We knew we needed to win today and we did that, we fight and that is good. View image in fullscreen Melvine Malard celebrates her goal against Everton. Photograph: Charlotte Tattersall/WSL/WSL Football/Getty Images Share

23m ago 10.25 EDT Manchester City striker Khadija Shaw tells Sky Sports: “It was a good afternoon, I must say. Especially off the back of the result last week, we knew we needed to have a great start and coming out here we did that. Overall a good performance.” Share

35m ago 10.12 EDT Khadija Shaw scored the fastest hat-trick in WSL history today. She netted three against Tottenham in just 12 minutes and 37 seconds. View image in fullscreen Khadija Shaw celebrates scoring her third goal against Tottenham. Photograph: Craig Brough/Action Images/Reuters Share

London City Lionesses 1-1 Chelsea

Arsenal 5-0 West Ham

Manchester United 2-1 Everton Share
